---
name: Feature Request / Enhancement
about: >-
  Propose a new message type, primitive, extension point, or behavioural
  enhancement to the EGAP specification.
title: "[feature] "
labels: ["enhancement", "triage"]
assignees: []
---

<!--
Before filing: search open Issues and the proposals/ directory for existing EIPs
covering the same topic. If this is a substantial change, consider filing a
full EGAP Improvement Proposal (EIP) per GOVERNANCE.md Section 7 instead.
-->

## Summary

<!-- One sentence: what you want to add, and why it belongs in EGAP. -->

## Problem Statement

<!-- What gap in the current specification does this address?
     Stay grounded in EGAP's scope: governed Engine-to-Agent communication.
     If the proposal is outside that scope, note that and we may redirect it. -->

## Proposed Solution

<!-- Describe the change at the protocol level.
     What new message types, fields, or behaviours would be introduced?
     How does it interact with the five governance primitives (identity, authorization,
     audit, approvals, alerts)? -->

## Governance Primitive Affected / Introduced

- [ ] Identity
- [ ] Authorization
- [ ] Audit
- [ ] Approvals
- [ ] Alerts
- [ ] New primitive (describe below)
- [ ] None (structural / transport / schema change)

## Alternatives Considered

<!-- What other approaches did you consider and why did you reject them? -->

## Backward Compatibility

<!-- Is this a breaking change? Can existing conformant implementations continue to function?
     Breaking changes require a major version increment per GOVERNANCE.md Section 6.5. -->

- [ ] Non-breaking (additive extension)
- [ ] Breaking (requires major version bump)
- [ ] Uncertain

## Sovereign / Air-Gap Compatibility

<!-- EGAP's principle: "Sovereignty by default." Does this change work in air-gapped environments?
     If it requires outbound internet calls, external services, or managed infrastructure, explain. -->

- [ ] Compatible with air-gapped deployments
- [ ] Requires external connectivity (explain below)

## Additional Context

<!-- Anything else: prior art, related standards (MCP, A2A, OTel), use cases, example flows. -->

---
